description_long: This is a classic, upright fire hydrant painted in a bright red color, commonly found in urban environments for emergency water access. The hydrant features a domed top with a central operating nut, flanked by two large hose connection outlets on either side and a smaller outlet on the front. The cylindrical body is reinforced with raised bands at the top and bottom, and the outlets are capped with hexagonal nuts. The surface shows some signs of wear, with minor scuffs and paint chipping, indicating exposure to outdoor conditions. The hydrant is constructed primarily from cast metal, likely iron or steel, designed to withstand high water pressure and physical impacts. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant is not shown in relation to any curb or sidewalk.
description: A red, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top, two large side outlets, and a smaller front outlet, all capped with hexagonal nuts. The surface is slightly worn, and the hydrant is made of heavy cast metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed top, central operating nut, and the smaller front outlet with its cap.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both large hose connection outlets and the central operating nut, with all three outlets visible.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view highlighting the side outlet and the cylindrical body, with the front outlet partially visible.
description_view_3: Back view showing the two large side outlets and the rear of the cylindrical body, with no front outlet visible.
